concepts.name: gravitomotive_transmission
concepts.definition: A theoretical model that describes a black hole as a self-sustaining, non-linear geometric structure of spacetime where angular momentum is imprinted onto the geometry via the Kerr metric, forming a 'gear train' known as the gravitomagnetic field.
concepts.insight: The model presents a new way to understand black holes as a unified, organic transmission of angular momentum from the singularity to the edge of causality, facilitated by the gravitomagnetic gear train of frame-dragged spacetime.

concepts.name: domestic_insights
concepts.definition: The idea that great insights in physics can be inspired by domestic, mundane objects or situations, as demonstrated by the analogy between a washing machine and a Kerr black hole.
concepts.insight: This concept challenges the traditional view of scientific discoveries being the result of rigorous academic research and instead highlights the role of intuition, creativity, and everyday experiences in the process of discovery.
